title: rosa hakonensis var. onoei
intro: Rosa hakonensis var. onoei, also known as Onoe's rose or Hakone rose, is a rare, deciduous shrub native to Japan, characterized by its fragrant flowers and edible fruits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate pain and prevent infections. Rosa hakonensis var. onoei is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, improving circulation, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote overall well-being and treat specific health conditions.
